description: Starting in late 2021, Starbucks workers in Buffalo, NY, successfully voted to unionize, sparking a wave of organization across the US. The campaign became a focal point of the 'Gen Z labor movement,' with intense public interest and debate regarding corporate anti-union tactics.

key_events.description: First Starbucks store successfully unionizes in Buffalo.
Reports of Starbucks firing union organizers surface.
NLRB files complaints against Starbucks for anti-union activity.
Public support peaks as the movement spreads nationwide.

calibration_notes: Models how exposure to 'union-busting' narratives can harden public support for a fledgling labor movement.
